mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 137,000 jobs indicates robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in triggered periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.01) with 95% confidence, or PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in quarterly GDP and monthly housing starts signals strong aggregate demand, increasing corporate revenue visibility and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST shows negative or insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in regression models, or 60-day SPX returns underperform by >2% in combined growth scenarios.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.13% triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing bond yields and reducing equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in triggered periods exceed 1.5% with 90% confidence, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r > -0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improved manufacturing activity, increasing corporate profits and investor risk appetite, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.10) or 20-day SPX returns underperform by >1.5% in high INDPRO regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates above 5.0% indicate slack in the labor market, reducing consumer spending and corporate pricing power, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 1% with 95% confidence, or UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and unemployment rate signal accommodative monetary policy and a tightening labor market, creating tailwinds for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE shows negative or insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in regression models, or 20-day SPX returns underperform by >1.5% in combined decline scenarios.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending as measured by retail sales (RSAFS) above 378,148 million dollars indicates strong demand for goods and services, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in triggered periods show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.005) with 95% confidence, or RSAFS fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.15).
